summary: A TED speaker profile for AI pioneer Fei-Fei Li, outlining her biographical details, her leadership at the Stanford Institute for Human-Centered AI, and her foundational work in teaching computers to see.

principles.statement: AI research, education, and policy must be guided by the goal of improving the human condition.
principles.rationale: She founded the Stanford Institute for Human-Centered AI specifically to ensure that the advancement of artificial intelligence remains focused on human-centric outcomes.

mental_models.name: AI as a Toddler
mental_models.description: A metaphor used to contextualize the current state of computer vision by comparing the visual recognition skills of advanced machines to the developmental stage of a young child.
mental_models.example: Noting that while a 3-year-old can effortlessly identify a cat, house, or plane by sight, our most advanced machines are still like toddlers when it comes to visual understanding.
